name: dumbing-us-down
description: John Taylor Gatto's classic critique — recognizing the hidden curriculum of compulsory schooling, the seven lessons school actually teaches, and reclaiming real education through self-direction, community, and family.
useCase: Understanding the hidden curriculum, deciding whether to homeschool/unschool, teaching guerrilla-style within the system, rebuilding family community, creating self-directed learning environments.
knownRisks: Gatto's position is radically anti-institutional — some readers will disagree fundamentally
Leaving the school system is not equally available to all families (financial, legal, work constraints)
Gatto's historical claims about pre-compulsory literacy are contested by some historians
